Name: Yale Undergraduate Aerospace Association (YUAA)
Relevance: Premier undergraduate aerospace engineering, rocketry, and aviation organization at Yale.

Name: Yale Drop Team
Relevance: Student team that designed and conducted microgravity experiments on complex plasmas aboard NASA's reduced-gravity aircraft.

Name: Yale Astronomical & Space Student Society (YASSS)
Relevance: Undergraduate organization dedicated to building a community for astronomy majors and space enthusiasts via stargazing and events.

Name: Yale Center for Astronomy and Astrophysics (YCAA)
Relevance: Interdisciplinary research center spanning the Physics and Astronomy departments focused on dark matter, cosmology, and the universe's evolution.

Name: Yale Wright Laboratory
Relevance: Core facility supporting research in particle physics, astrophysics, and the development of advanced instrumentation for dark matter and neutrino studies.

Name: Yale Exoplanet Group
Relevance: Research group that studies exoplanets and built the Extreme Precision Spectrometer (EXPRES) to search for Earth-like planets.

Name: Yale Center for the Invisible Universe (YCIU)
Relevance: Research center dedicated to exploring fundamental cosmic forces through the study of dark matter, dark energy, black holes, and neutrinos.

Name: Earth Observation Lab (EOL)
Relevance: Consolidates satellite remote sensing and UAV technologies for geospatial research under the Yale Center for Geospatial Solutions.

Name: U.S. Space Command Academic Engagement Enterprise
Relevance: Partners with US Space Command to shape the future space workforce, providing Yale students with mentorship and space applied research opportunities.

Name: AIAA Yale Student Branch
Relevance: Yale student branch of the world's largest technical aerospace society, providing mentorship and aerospace engineering opportunities.

Name: Department of Astronomy
Relevance: Academic department offering extensive research and coursework in astrophysics, cosmology, and planetary science.

Name: Leitner Family Observatory and Planetarium
Relevance: On-campus observatory and digital planetarium facilitating undergraduate research, coursework, and public outreach in astronomy.

Name: The GRAB Lab
Relevance: Develops compliant robotic grippers allowing Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) to hover, grasp, and manipulate objects in mid-air.

Name: Yale Faboratory
Relevance: Develops soft robotics, intelligent materials, and adaptive systems which hold potential for advanced aerospace and extraterrestrial surface exploration, though not explicitly space-focused.

Name: Bulldog Racing
Relevance: Formula SAE team focused on automotive racing; involves highly transferable aerospace skills such as complex mechanical design, aerodynamics, and composite materials.

Name: Chem-E-Car
Relevance: Designs small-scale chemically powered vehicles; involves power generation, chemical reactions, and fluid dynamics akin to chemical rocketry principles.

Name: FIRST at Yale (FaY)
Relevance: Supports FIRST robotics teams and competitions; provides foundational engineering and robotics experience that strongly correlates with aerospace career pathways.
